_comment: D3+D4 law library (plan0619.md §D3). Each law maps a structural trigger (applies_when) to a hard physical constraint that a candidate must satisfy before the expensive solver is invoked. The law engine derives all geometry from the DeviceTree region boxes (Candidate.params is unreliable after structural/mechanism mutations — see law_engine.extract_geometry).

laws.name: Negative-capacitance stability window
laws.effect: FE layer across the gate dielectric yields NC voltage gain only inside the stability window

constraints.msg: t_fe/tox outside [1, 5]: NC unstable (too thin FE loses polarization, too thick decouples)
kappa_fe outside [20, 50]: outside the stable NC gain window

laws.rationale: A ferroelectric inserted between the oxide and gate metal acts as a negative capacitor only when its thickness is commensurate with the EOT (t_fe/tox in [1,5]) and its permittivity sits in the HfZrO-family NC window (kappa in [20,50]). Outside this window the FE either loses spontaneous polarization (too thin) or decouples from the channel (too thick / too low kappa).

laws.name: Dirac-source band-pass filter
laws.effect: Graphene (Dirac) source + bandgap channel yields steep-slope band-pass filtering

constraints.msg: channel Eg < 0.8 eV with Dirac source: no band-pass window

laws.rationale: A Dirac (graphene) source achieves sub-60mV/dec switching by filtering the Fermi tail through a channel bandgap. The filter only works when the channel Eg is large enough (>0.8 eV) to carve a transmission window; a narrow-gap channel lets the tail through and destroys steepness.

laws.name: High-k dielectric interface sanity
laws.effect: High-k gate dielectric is only beneficial at thin EOT

constraints.msg: high-k kappa outside [5, 40]: excessive interface states / phonon scattering
tox > 1.5nm with high-k: EOT too thick, high-k brings no benefit

laws.rationale: High-k dielectrics above kappa~40 suffer from soft optical phonons and interface traps that degrade mobility; below kappa~5 they are no better than SiO2. The capacitance benefit only materializes at thin EOT (tox <= 1.5nm); a thick high-k layer is dominated by remote phonon scattering with no gate-control gain.

laws.name: Gate length physical floor
laws.effect: Below ~5nm the drift-diffusion / DG model loses validity (source-drain tunneling dominates)

constraints.msg: Lg < 5nm: below the classical DD validity floor (source-drain tunneling dominates)

laws.rationale: The _PARAM_SPECS table floors Lg at 5nm for parameter mutation, but structural mutations (gate_wrap) and open-space mechanism mutations can bypass it. This law is the physical backstop: below 5nm the drift-diffusion + density-gradient model the solver uses is no longer predictive (direct source-drain tunneling dominates), so evaluating such a candidate wastes solver budget on non-credible physics.
